structure_description: HfSiTe crystallizes in the tetragonal P4/nmm space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one HfSiTe sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Hf(1) is bonded in a 8-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Si(1) and four equivalent Te(1) atoms. All Hf(1)-Si(1) bond lengths are 2.79 Å. All Hf(1)-Te(1) bond lengths are 2.95 Å. Si(1) is bonded in a 8-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Hf(1) and four equivalent Si(1) atoms. All Si(1)-Si(1) bond lengths are 2.59 Å. Te(1) is bonded in a 4-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Hf(1) atoms.

As crystallizes in the orthorhombic Pmna space group. The structure is zero-dimensional and consists of two As clusters. As(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one As(1) atom. The As(1)-As(1) bond length is 2.50 Å.

TiOBr crystallizes in the orthorhombic Pmmn space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one TiOBr sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Ti(1) is bonded to four equivalent O(1) and two equivalent Br(1) atoms to form a mixture of distorted corner and edge-sharing TiBr2O4 octahedra. The corner-sharing octahedral tilt angles are 6°. There are two shorter (2.02 Å) and two longer (2.06 Å) Ti(1)-O(1) bond lengths. Both Ti(1)-Br(1) bond lengths are 2.59 Å. O(1) is bonded in a see-saw-like geometry to four equivalent Ti(1) atoms. Br(1) is bonded in an L-shaped geometry to two equivalent Ti(1) atoms.

InBi crystallizes in the tetragonal P4/nmm space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one InBi sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. In(1) is bonded to four equivalent Bi(1) atoms to form a mixture of distorted corner and edge-sharing InBi4 tetrahedra. All In(1)-Bi(1) bond lengths are 3.07 Å. Bi(1) is bonded in a 4-coordinate geometry to four equivalent In(1) atoms.

NbO2 is Molybdenite-like structured and crystallizes in the hexagonal P-6m2 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one NbO2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Nb(1) is bonded in a 6-coordinate geometry to six equivalent O(1) atoms. All Nb(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 2.10 Å. O(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent Nb(1) atoms.

SbAsO3 crystallizes in the triclinic P1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one SbAsO3 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Sb(1,2) is bonded in a 4-coordinate geometry to one O(2), one O(3), and two equivalent O(1) atoms. The Sb(1,2)-O(2) bond length is 2.04 Å. The Sb(1,2)-O(3) bond length is 2.01 Å. There is one shorter (2.06 Å) and one longer (2.64 Å) Sb(1,2)-O(1) bond length. As(1,2) is bonded in a 1-coordinate geometry to one O(1) and one O(4) atom. The As(1,2)-O(1) bond length is 1.83 Å. The As(1,2)-O(4) bond length is 1.82 Å. There are six inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a 3-coordinate geometry to two equivalent Sb(1,2) and one As(1,2) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Sb(1,2) atom. In the third O site, O(3) is bonded in a bent 120 degrees geometry to one Sb(1,2) and one As(1,2) atom. The O(3)-As(1,2) bond length is 1.82 Å. In the fourth O site, O(4) is bonded in a bent 120 degrees geometry to one Sb(1,2) and one As(1,2) atom. The O(4)-Sb(1,2) bond length is 2.01 Å. In the fifth O site, O(5) is bonded in a 3-coordinate geometry to two equivalent Sb(1,2) and one As(1,2) atom. There is one shorter (2.06 Å) and one longer (2.64 Å) O(5)-Sb(1,2) bond length. The O(5)-As(1,2) bond length is 1.83 Å. In the sixth O site, O(6) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Sb(1,2) atom. The O(6)-Sb(1,2) bond length is 2.04 Å.

MoO3 crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one MoO3 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Mo(1) is bonded in a 6-coordinate geometry to one O(1), two equivalent O(3), and three equivalent O(2) atoms. The Mo(1)-O(1) bond length is 1.68 Å. There is one shorter (1.77 Å) and one longer (2.16 Å) Mo(1)-O(3) bond length. There are two shorter (1.95 Å) and one longer (2.44 Å) Mo(1)-O(2) bond length. There are three inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Mo(1) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a 3-coordinate geometry to three equivalent Mo(1) atoms. In the third O site, O(3) is bonded in a distorted linear geometry to two equivalent Mo(1) atoms.

CrGeTe3 crystallizes in the trigonal P-31m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one CrGeTe3 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Cr(1) is bonded to six equivalent Te(1) atoms to form CrTe6 octahedra that share corners with six equivalent Ge(1)GeTe3 tetrahedra and edges with three equivalent Cr(1)Te6 octahedra. All Cr(1)-Te(1) bond lengths are 2.78 Å. Ge(1) is bonded to one Ge(1) and three equivalent Te(1) atoms to form GeGeTe3 tetrahedra that share corners with six equivalent Cr(1)Te6 octahedra. The corner-sharing octahedral tilt angles are 79°. The Ge(1)-Ge(1) bond length is 2.42 Å. All Ge(1)-Te(1) bond lengths are 2.63 Å. Te(1) is bonded in a 3-coordinate geometry to two equivalent Cr(1) and one Ge(1) atom.

ZrNBr crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one ZrNBr sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Zr(1) is bonded in a 7-coordinate geometry to four equivalent N(1) and three equivalent Br(1) atoms. There are three shorter (2.16 Å) and one longer (2.18 Å) Zr(1)-N(1) bond length. All Zr(1)-Br(1) bond lengths are 2.90 Å. N(1) is bonded to four equivalent Zr(1) atoms to form a mixture of corner and edge-sharing NZr4 tetrahedra. Br(1) is bonded in a 3-coordinate geometry to three equivalent Zr(1) atoms.

PdSeO3 crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/m space group. The structure is one-dimensional and consists of one PdSeO3 ribbon oriented in the (0, 1, 0) direction. Pd(1) is bonded in a square co-planar geometry to two equivalent O(1) and two equivalent O(2) atoms. Both Pd(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 2.04 Å. Both Pd(1)-O(2) bond lengths are 2.02 Å. Se(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one O(2) atom. The Se(1)-O(2) bond length is 1.84 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Pd(1) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a trigonal non-coplanar geometry to two equivalent Pd(1) and one Se(1) atom.

NbOBr2 crystallizes in the orthorhombic Pmm2 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one NbOBr2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Nb(1) is bonded in a distorted linear geometry to two equivalent O(1), two equivalent Br(1), and two equivalent Br(2) atoms. There is one shorter (1.87 Å) and one longer (2.06 Å) Nb(1)-O(1) bond length. Both Nb(1)-Br(1) bond lengths are 2.61 Å. Both Nb(1)-Br(2) bond lengths are 2.70 Å. O(1) is bonded in a linear geometry to two equivalent Nb(1) atoms. There are two inequivalent Br sites. In the first Br site, Br(1) is bonded in a 2-coordinate geometry to two equivalent Nb(1) atoms. In the second Br site, Br(2) is bonded in an L-shaped geometry to two equivalent Nb(1) atoms.

SrSbSe2F crystallizes in the tetragonal P4/nmm space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one SrSbSe2F sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Sr(1) is bonded in a 8-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Se(2) and four equivalent F(1) atoms. All Sr(1)-Se(2) bond lengths are 3.18 Å. All Sr(1)-F(1) bond lengths are 2.51 Å. Sb(1) is bonded in a distorted single-bond geometry to one Se(2) and four equivalent Se(1) atoms. The Sb(1)-Se(2) bond length is 2.55 Å. All Sb(1)-Se(1) bond lengths are 2.93 Å. There are two inequivalent Se sites. In the first Se site, Se(1) is bonded in a square co-planar geometry to four equivalent Sb(1) atoms. In the second Se site, Se(2) is bonded in a 5-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Sr(1) and one Sb(1) atom. F(1) is bonded to four equivalent Sr(1) atoms to form a mixture of corner and edge-sharing FSr4 tetrahedra.

Lix0CoO2 is trigonal omega-like structured and crystallizes in the monoclinic C2/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Lix0CoO2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Co(1) is bonded to six equivalent O(1) atoms to form edge-sharing CoO6 octahedra. All Co(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.88 Å. O(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent Co(1) atoms.

AgI crystallizes in the tetragonal P4/nmm space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one AgI sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Ag(1) is bonded in a 4-coordinate geometry to four equivalent I(1) atoms. All Ag(1)-I(1) bond lengths are 2.92 Å. I(1) is bonded in a 4-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Ag(1) atoms.

CoI2 is trigonal omega-like structured and crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one CoI2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Co(1) is bonded to six equivalent I(1) atoms to form edge-sharing CoI6 octahedra. All Co(1)-I(1) bond lengths are 2.74 Å. I(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent Co(1) atoms.

NbSe2Br2 crystallizes in the monoclinic C2/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one NbSe2Br2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Nb(1) is bonded in a 8-coordinate geometry to two equivalent Se(1), two equivalent Se(2), and four equivalent Br(1) atoms. Both Nb(1)-Se(1) bond lengths are 2.64 Å. Both Nb(1)-Se(2) bond lengths are 2.66 Å. There are two shorter (2.77 Å) and two longer (2.79 Å) Nb(1)-Br(1) bond lengths. There are two inequivalent Se sites. In the first Se site, Se(1) is bonded in a 2-coordinate geometry to two equivalent Nb(1) atoms. In the second Se site, Se(2) is bonded in a 2-coordinate geometry to two equivalent Nb(1) atoms. Br(1) is bonded in a water-like geometry to two equivalent Nb(1) atoms.

ZrNBr crystallizes in the orthorhombic Pmmn space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one ZrNBr sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Zr(1) is bonded in a 6-coordinate geometry to four equivalent N(1) and two equivalent Br(1) atoms. There are two shorter (2.15 Å) and two longer (2.16 Å) Zr(1)-N(1) bond lengths. Both Zr(1)-Br(1) bond lengths are 2.77 Å. N(1) is bonded to four equivalent Zr(1) atoms to form a mixture of distorted corner and edge-sharing NZr4 tetrahedra. Br(1) is bonded in an L-shaped geometry to two equivalent Zr(1) atoms.

InBr3 is Chromium trichloride-like structured and crystallizes in the monoclinic C2/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one InBr3 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. In(1) is bonded to six equivalent Br(1,2) atoms to form edge-sharing InBr6 octahedra. All In(1)-Br(1,2) bond lengths are 2.72 Å. Br(1,2) is bonded in an L-shaped geometry to two equivalent In(1) atoms.

YI(Ga) crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one YI(Ga) sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Y(1) is bonded to six equivalent Ga(1) and three equivalent I(1) atoms to form a mixture of distorted face and edge-sharing YGa6I3 octahedra. There are three shorter (2.93 Å) and three longer (3.38 Å) Y(1)-Ga(1) bond lengths. All Y(1)-I(1) bond lengths are 3.13 Å. Ga(1) is bonded in a 9-coordinate geometry to six equivalent Y(1) and three equivalent Ga(1) atoms. All Ga(1)-Ga(1) bond lengths are 2.52 Å. I(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent Y(1) atoms.

YI3 is Bismuth triodide-like structured and crystallizes in the trigonal P-31m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one YI3 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Y(1) is bonded to six equivalent I(1) atoms to form edge-sharing YI6 octahedra. All Y(1)-I(1) bond lengths are 3.03 Å. I(1) is bonded in an L-shaped geometry to two equivalent Y(1) atoms.

NbCoTe2 crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/c space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one NbCoTe2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Nb(1) is bonded in a 9-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Co(1), two equivalent Te(2), and three equivalent Te(1) atoms. There are a spread of Nb(1)-Co(1) bond distances ranging from 2.60-2.94 Å. There is one shorter (2.83 Å) and one longer (2.89 Å) Nb(1)-Te(2) bond length. There are a spread of Nb(1)-Te(1) bond distances ranging from 2.83-3.01 Å. Co(1) is bonded in a 9-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Nb(1), one Co(1), two equivalent Te(1), and two equivalent Te(2) atoms. The Co(1)-Co(1) bond length is 2.45 Å. There is one shorter (2.62 Å) and one longer (2.66 Å) Co(1)-Te(1) bond length. There is one shorter (2.55 Å) and one longer (2.59 Å) Co(1)-Te(2) bond length. There are two inequivalent Te sites. In the first Te site, Te(1) is bonded in a 5-coordinate geometry to three equivalent Nb(1) and two equivalent Co(1) atoms. In the second Te site, Te(2) is bonded in a 4-coordinate geometry to two equivalent Nb(1) and two equivalent Co(1) atoms.

MnSe crystallizes in the tetragonal P4/nmm space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one MnSe sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Mn(1) is bonded to four equivalent Se(1) atoms to form a mixture of distorted corner and edge-sharing MnSe4 tetrahedra. All Mn(1)-Se(1) bond lengths are 2.38 Å. Se(1) is bonded in a 4-coordinate geometry to four equivalent Mn(1) atoms.

AlOOH crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one AlOOH sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Al(1) is bonded to two equivalent O(1) and four equivalent O(2) atoms to form a mixture of corner and edge-sharing AlO6 octahedra. The corner-sharing octahedral tilt angles are 23°. Both Al(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.90 Å. There are two shorter (1.89 Å) and two longer (1.95 Å) Al(1)-O(2) bond lengths. H(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one O(1) atom. The H(1)-O(1) bond length is 0.97 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a distorted trigonal planar geometry to two equivalent Al(1) and one H(1) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded to four equivalent Al(1) atoms to form a mixture of corner and edge-sharing OAl4 trigonal pyramids.

Lix0CoO2 is trigonal omega-like structured and crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Lix0CoO2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Co(1) is bonded to six equivalent O(1) atoms to form edge-sharing CoO6 octahedra. All Co(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.88 Å. O(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent Co(1) atoms.

BiBr3 is Bismuth triodide-like structured and crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/c space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one BiBr3 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Bi(1) is bonded to six equivalent Br(1,2,3) atoms to form edge-sharing BiBr6 octahedra. All Bi(1)-Br(1,2,3) bond lengths are 2.90 Å. Br(1,2,3) is bonded in an L-shaped geometry to two equivalent Bi(1) atoms.

YOBr crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one YOBr sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Y(1) is bonded in a 7-coordinate geometry to four equivalent O(1) and three equivalent Br(1) atoms. All Y(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 2.29 Å. All Y(1)-Br(1) bond lengths are 2.93 Å. O(1) is bonded to four equivalent Y(1) atoms to form a mixture of corner and edge-sharing OY4 tetrahedra. Br(1) is bonded in a 3-coordinate geometry to three equivalent Y(1) atoms.

Mg(ReO4)2 crystallizes in the trigonal P-3 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Mg(ReO4)2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Mg(1) is bonded to six equivalent O(1) atoms to form MgO6 octahedra that share corners with six equivalent Re(1)O4 tetrahedra. All Mg(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 2.07 Å. Re(1) is bonded to one O(2) and three equivalent O(1) atoms to form ReO4 tetrahedra that share corners with three equivalent Mg(1)O6 octahedra. The corner-sharing octahedral tilt angles are 21°. The Re(1)-O(2) bond length is 1.72 Å. All Re(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.76 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a bent 150 degrees geometry to one Mg(1) and one Re(1) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Re(1) atom.

BiOF is Cotunnite-derived structured and crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one BiOF sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Bi(1) is bonded in a 7-coordinate geometry to four equivalent O(1) and three equivalent F(1) atoms. There is one shorter (2.25 Å) and three longer (2.40 Å) Bi(1)-O(1) bond lengths. All Bi(1)-F(1) bond lengths are 2.49 Å. O(1) is bonded to four equivalent Bi(1) atoms to form a mixture of corner and edge-sharing OBi4 tetrahedra. F(1) is bonded in a trigonal non-coplanar geometry to three equivalent Bi(1) atoms.

NiTeO4 is trigonal omega-derived structured and crystallizes in the monoclinic P2/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one NiTeO4 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Ni(1) is bonded to two equivalent O(2) and four equivalent O(1) atoms to form NiO6 octahedra that share edges with two equivalent Ni(1)O6 octahedra and edges with four equivalent Te(1)O6 octahedra. Both Ni(1)-O(2) bond lengths are 2.12 Å. All Ni(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 2.05 Å. Te(1) is bonded to two equivalent O(1) and four equivalent O(2) atoms to form TeO6 octahedra that share edges with two equivalent Te(1)O6 octahedra and edges with four equivalent Ni(1)O6 octahedra. Both Te(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.92 Å. All Te(1)-O(2) bond lengths are 2.05 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a distorted trigonal non-coplanar geometry to two equivalent Ni(1) and one Te(1) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to one Ni(1) and two equivalent Te(1) atoms.

TiZrTe4 is trigonal omega-derived structured and crystallizes in the monoclinic P2/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one TiZrTe4 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Zr(1) is bonded to two equivalent Te(1) and four equivalent Te(2) atoms to form ZrTe6 octahedra that share edges with two equivalent Zr(1)Te6 octahedra and edges with four equivalent Ti(1)Te6 octahedra. Both Zr(1)-Te(1) bond lengths are 2.89 Å. All Zr(1)-Te(2) bond lengths are 2.90 Å. Ti(1) is bonded to two equivalent Te(2) and four equivalent Te(1) atoms to form TiTe6 octahedra that share edges with two equivalent Ti(1)Te6 octahedra and edges with four equivalent Zr(1)Te6 octahedra. Both Ti(1)-Te(2) bond lengths are 2.82 Å. All Ti(1)-Te(1) bond lengths are 2.80 Å. There are two inequivalent Te sites. In the first Te site, Te(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to one Zr(1) and two equivalent Ti(1) atoms. In the second Te site, Te(2) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to two equivalent Zr(1) and one Ti(1) atom.

Si3H crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Si3H sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. There are three inequivalent Si sites. In the first Si site, Si(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to three equivalent Si(2) and one H(1) atom. All Si(1)-Si(2) bond lengths are 2.37 Å. The Si(1)-H(1) bond length is 1.51 Å. In the second Si site, Si(2) is bonded to one Si(3) and three equivalent Si(1) atoms to form corner-sharing SiSi4 tetrahedra. The Si(2)-Si(3) bond length is 2.39 Å. In the third Si site, Si(3) is bonded to one Si(2) and three equivalent Si(3) atoms to form corner-sharing SiSi4 tetrahedra. All Si(3)-Si(3) bond lengths are 2.38 Å. H(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Si(1) atom.

Lix0CoO2 is Cyanogen Chloride-derived structured and crystallizes in the hexagonal P6/mmm space group. The structure is zero-dimensional and consists of one Lix0CoO2 cluster. Co(1) is bonded in a linear geometry to two equivalent O(1) atoms. Both Co(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.59 Å. O(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Co(1) atom.

VO2 is trigonal omega-like structured and crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one VO2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. V(1) is bonded to six equivalent O(1) atoms to form edge-sharing VO6 octahedra. All V(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.94 Å. O(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent V(1) atoms.

TeO3 crystallizes in the hexagonal P-6m2 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one TeO3 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Te(1) is bonded to two equivalent O(1) and three equivalent O(2) atoms to form corner-sharing TeO5 trigonal bipyramids. Both Te(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.80 Å. All Te(1)-O(2) bond lengths are 2.17 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Te(1) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a trigonal planar geometry to three equivalent Te(1) atoms.

BiO2 crystallizes in the hexagonal P-6m2 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one BiO2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Bi(1) is bonded in a 6-coordinate geometry to six equivalent O(1) atoms. All Bi(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 2.40 Å. O(1) is bonded in a distorted trigonal non-coplanar geometry to three equivalent Bi(1) atoms.

Ho2C is trigonal omega-like structured and crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Ho2C sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Ho(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent C(1) atoms. All Ho(1)-C(1) bond lengths are 2.43 Å. C(1) is bonded to six equivalent Ho(1) atoms to form edge-sharing CHo6 octahedra.

Zr2PTe2 crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Zr2PTe2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Zr(1) is bonded to three equivalent P(1) and three equivalent Te(1) atoms to form a mixture of corner and edge-sharing ZrTe3P3 octahedra. The corner-sharing octahedra are not tilted. All Zr(1)-P(1) bond lengths are 2.65 Å. All Zr(1)-Te(1) bond lengths are 2.92 Å. P(1) is bonded to six equivalent Zr(1) atoms to form edge-sharing PZr6 octahedra. Te(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent Zr(1) atoms.

TeO2 crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/c space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one TeO2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Te(1) is bonded to two equivalent O(1) and three equivalent O(2) atoms to form a mixture of distorted corner and edge-sharing TeO5 square pyramids. There is one shorter (1.95 Å) and one longer (2.03 Å) Te(1)-O(1) bond length. There are a spread of Te(1)-O(2) bond distances ranging from 1.93-2.50 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a bent 150 degrees geometry to two equivalent Te(1) atoms. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a 3-coordinate geometry to three equivalent Te(1) atoms.

LuHCl crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one LuHCl sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Lu(1) is bonded in a 7-coordinate geometry to four equivalent H(1) and three equivalent Cl(1) atoms. There is one shorter (2.12 Å) and three longer (2.15 Å) Lu(1)-H(1) bond lengths. All Lu(1)-Cl(1) bond lengths are 2.68 Å. H(1) is bonded to four equivalent Lu(1) atoms to form a mixture of corner and edge-sharing HLu4 tetrahedra. Cl(1) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to three equivalent Lu(1) atoms.

Te4O9 crystallizes in the trigonal P-3 space group. The structure is zero-dimensional and consists of six TeO clusters and two TeO6 clusters. In each TeO cluster, Te(2) is bonded in a distorted single-bond geometry to one O(3) atom. The Te(2)-O(3) bond length is 1.92 Å. O(3) is bonded in a distorted single-bond geometry to one Te(2) atom. In each TeO6 cluster, Te(1) is bonded in an octahedral geometry to three equivalent O(1) and three equivalent O(2) atoms. All Te(1)-O(1) bond lengths are 1.93 Å. All Te(1)-O(2) bond lengths are 1.99 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Te(1) atom. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a single-bond geometry to one Te(1) atom.

UBr4 crystallizes in the monoclinic C2/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one UBr4 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Pr3I3Os crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/m space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Pr3I3Os sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. There are three inequivalent Pr sites. In the first Pr site, Pr(1) is bonded in a 5-coordinate geometry to one Os(1), two equivalent I(2), and two equivalent I(3) atoms. The corner-sharing octahedral tilt angles range from 2-9°. There are two shorter (2.91 Å) and one longer (2.94 Å) Pr(2)-Os(1) bond length. The Pr(2)-I(3) bond length is 3.44 Å. Both Pr(2)-I(1) bond lengths are 3.51 Å. In the third Pr site, Pr(3) is bonded to two equivalent Os(1), one I(2), and three equivalent I(1) atoms to form distorted PrOs2I4 octahedra that share corners with four equivalent Pr(2)Os3I3 octahedra, edges with four equivalent Pr(3)Os2I4 octahedra, and edges with four equivalent Pr(2)Os3I3 octahedra. The corner-sharing octahedral tilt angles range from 2-9°. Both Pr(3)-Os(1) bond lengths are 2.87 Å. The Pr(3)-I(2) bond length is 3.31 Å. There is one shorter (3.34 Å) and two longer (3.46 Å) Pr(3)-I(1) bond lengths. Os(1) is bonded to one Pr(1), two equivalent Pr(3), and three equivalent Pr(2) atoms to form OsPr6 octahedra that share corners with four equivalent I(1)Pr5 square pyramids, edges with four equivalent Os(1)Pr6 octahedra, and edges with four equivalent I(1)Pr5 square pyramids. There are three inequivalent I sites. In the first I site, I(1) is bonded to two equivalent Pr(2) and three equivalent Pr(3) atoms to form distorted IPr5 square pyramids that share corners with four equivalent Os(1)Pr6 octahedra, edges with four equivalent Os(1)Pr6 octahedra, and edges with four equivalent I(1)Pr5 square pyramids. The corner-sharing octahedral tilt angles range from 11-12°. In the second I site, I(2) is bonded in a 3-coordinate geometry to one Pr(3) and two equivalent Pr(1) atoms. In the third I site, I(3) is bonded in a distorted T-shaped geometry to one Pr(2) and two equivalent Pr(1) atoms.

BCl2 is Indium-like structured and crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/c space group. The structure is zero-dimensional and consists of four BCl2 clusters. UTe3 crystallizes in the orthorhombic Pmn2_1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one UTe3 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. ScHCl is Cotunnite-derived structured and crystallizes in the trigonal P-3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one ScHCl sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Nb3I8 crystallizes in the trigonal P3m1 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Nb3I8 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. TaCoTe2 crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/c space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one TaCoTe2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Co(ClO4)2 crystallizes in the trigonal P-3 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Co(ClO4)2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Ca2CuBr2O2 crystallizes in the tetragonal P4/mmm space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Ca2CuBr2O2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Pd(Se4Cl)2 crystallizes in the monoclinic P2_1/c space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one Pd(Se4Cl)2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. HgI2 crystallizes in the tetragonal P-4m2 space group. The structure is two-dimensional and consists of one HgI2 sheet oriented in the (0, 0, 1) direction. Lix0CoO2 is trigonal omega-like structured and crystallizes in the monoclinic C2/m space group.
